ref: While Joseph was thinking about this, an angel from the Lord appeared to him in a dream. The angel said, “Joseph, the baby that Mary will have is from the Holy Spirit. Go ahead and marry her.
text: Wʊn wa tɩɛr a yele nyã maalfʊ, ka a Nãaŋmɩn malaka wa a wʊ sɛ̃ jãn pɔ wa 'yɛr, “Josefʊ, David bie, ta joro dãbãɛ ɩka fʊ de a Mɛɛr ka wʊ ɩ a fʊ pɔɔ ɛ, a bie na be a wʊ pʊɔm yin a Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ kpɛ̃ɔ pɔ.
translated: When he was thinking about this, an angel of the Lord appeared to him in a dream, saying, "Joseph, son of David, do not be afraid to take Mary as your wife, because the child that is in her womb is from the Holy Spirit.

ref: The angel said, “Get up and take the child and his mother back to Israel. The people who wanted to kill him are now dead.”
text: “Vaa ir 'mɔɔ a bie nɩ a wʊ ma na fʊ chiin a Isɩral wɛr pɔ, a bala na dɔ̃ɔ bʊɔrɔ a bie kʊfʊ kpi naa.”
translated: If you do not drive out the inhabitants of the land before you, those you allow to remain will become barbs in your eyes and thorns in your sides.

ref: But when he heard that Herod's son Archelaus was now ruler of Judea, he was afraid to go there. Then in a dream he was told to go to Galilee,
text: Tɩchɛ wʊn wa wõ ɩka Archelaus lɛ di a nãa a Judiya a wʊ sãa kũu puor, wʊ joro na dãbãɛ ɩka wʊ lɛ chen a be. Ka a Nãaŋmɩn wa kpãa wʊ a jãn pɔ ɩka wʊ chen be Galilee paal pɔ.
translated: But when he heard that Archelaus was reigning over Judea in place of his father, he was afraid to go there. And God warned him in a dream so he went to the region of Galilee.

ref: He said, “Turn back to God! The kingdom of heaven will soon be here.”
text: 'yɛrɛ, “Yɩ lɩɛb a yɩ befʊ, a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ ta na.”
translated: saying, "You know and you refuse, and that is God's truth."

ref: From Jerusalem and all Judea and from the Jordan River Valley crowds of people went to John.
text: Nɩyɔɔ yin a Jerusalɛm nɩ Judiya chiine a wʊ sɛ̃ nɩ a bala na yi a Jordan man koor.
translated: And he went throughout all the region around the Jordan preaching in Jerusalem and Judea and all the surrounding area.

ref: Jesus answered, “The Scriptures also say, ‘Don't try to test the Lord your God!’ ”
text: Ka a Yesu mɩ 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, “A mɩ sɛb a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ ɩka, ta bɛɛr a yɩ Nãaŋmɩn kaa ɛ.” (Mosesɩ Gãma Na Tu Anũu
translated: If you believe in Jesus, "You will see the glory of God," he said to her.

ref: So God's promise came true, just as the prophet Isaiah had said,
text: Ka a lɛ jaa a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ Isaiya na dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr ɩ yelmãɛ,
translated: And you have fulfilled the prophecy of Isaiah which says,

ref: When you make a promise, say only “Yes” or “No.” Anything else comes from the devil. Revenge (Luke
text: Yɩ vɛ̃ ka a yɩ ɔ̃ɔ ɩ ɔ̃ɔ ka a yɩ kai ɩ kai, bon 'lɔ na gar a nɩtɔ̃ yin a dɛɛr sʊɔ sɛ̃.
translated: Whether you are a man or a woman, God will judge you according to your deeds.

ref: If you greet only your friends, what's so great about this? Don't even unbelievers do that?
text: Yɩn ma puoro a bala tɛɛ na ɩ a yɩ nɩbɛ, bo tɔ̃nɔ̃ lɛ be be? A bala na ba ɩ a Juu nɩbɛ ɛ, maa mɩ ɩ a lɛ bɩɩ?
translated: If I don't greet a person well or do something bad to them, will they be happy? If a person doesn't do good to you, will you be happy with them?

ref: But you must always act like your Father in heaven.
text: Yɩ ɩ nɩmɩn a lɛ a yɩ sãa Nãaŋmɩn na be a saaju kaar.
translated: If you believe in him, he will give you the kingdom of God.

ref: If you forgive others for the wrongs they do to you, your Father in heaven will forgive you.
text: “Yɩn wa vɛ̃ a yɩ taaba yeldɛbɛ bar ba, a yɩ Sãa Nãaŋmɩn na be a saaju mɩ na vɛ̃n bar yɩ.
translated: If you want to be his disciple, you must be willing to let God use you to help others.

ref: Don't worry about tomorrow. It will take care of itself. You have enough to worry about today.
text: A lɛ jũu, yɩ ta waala a bio 'yɔ̃ɔ ɛ, bio lɛ na waala a wʊ mãɛ 'yɔ̃ɔ. Bɩbir jaa tara na a wʊ yelwõnsɩ.
translated: If it is good, you should do it for your child, the child will also do it for his/her own child. Good things will continue in your family/lineage.

ref: Don't store up treasures on earth! Moths and rust can destroy them, and thieves can break in and steal them.
text: “Yɩ ta bine a yɩ nãbonsɩ ka a wɛr ju be kpolo, nɩ sɔrɔ, nɩ na sɔ̃ɔ bar ɛ, nɩ be nãnyisɩ nɩ naa kpɛ ju ɛ.
translated: If a person is a sinner and does not repent, and dies, and does not believe in him, and the judgment comes upon him.

ref: You cannot be the slave of two masters! You will like one more than the other or be more loyal to one than the other. You cannot serve both God and money. Worry (Luke
text: “Nɩrɛ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ tʊ̃ nɩbɛrɛ ayi tʊ̃mɔ̃ ɛ. Wʊn nʊ̃n ãsʊɔ, tɩ 'laar ãsʊɔ, bɩɩ wõnõ a ãsʊɔ toor, tɩ ba wõnõ ãsʊɔ toor ɛ. Yɩ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ tʊ̃ kʊ Nãaŋmɩn nɩ libie ɛ.
translated: A person should not do things that are not good. If you do something, whether it's a good thing, or even if you do a bad thing, whether they do a bad thing. It is a thing that God and people see.

ref: But I tell you that Solomon with all his wealth wasn't as well clothed as one of them.
text: a Nãa Solomon gba nɩ a wʊ tɩɩr wʊ jaa ba dɔ̃ɔ su fuu ka wʊ vɩɛlɛ gar a mɔpuul a nyãna ɛ.
translated: And King Solomon gave to the queen of Sheba all that she desired and asked for besides what he gave her of his royal bounty.

ref: God gives such beauty to everything that grows in the fields, even though it is here today and thrown into a fire tomorrow. God will surely do even more for you! Why do you have such little faith?
text: Ala ɩ Nãaŋmɩn maal a mɔpuul ka a vɩɛlɛ, tɩchɛ a maa kor ɛ. Nɩbɛ ma chʊm ka a di. A Nãaŋmɩn na ma maal a mɔpuul a nɩtɔ̃, wʊ kaara a yɩn na ɩ a wʊ nɩbɛ gar a mɔpuul anyãna. A yɩ sɔɔfʊ lɛ ba jã bon ɛ.
translated: But if God clothes the grass of the field, which today exists and tomorrow is thrown into the oven, will he not much more clothe you, O you of little faith?

ref: Everyone who asks will receive. Everyone who searches will find. And the door will be opened for everyone who knocks.
text: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ na sʊɔr na nyɛ naa, ka a 'lɔ mɩ na yɔbɔ nyɛ, ka a pãn mɩ yuo kʊ a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na kpoor.
translated: If someone wants to do something bad, and he tells me to help him, and he gives me money for his bad thing to succeed.

ref: Not everyone who calls me their Lord will get into the kingdom of heaven. Only the ones who obey my Father in heaven will get in.
text: “A ba ɩ nɩrɛ jaa na bʊɔlɔ ɩka n Soro, n Soro, lɛ na kpɛ a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ pɔ ɛ, tɩchɛ nɩrɛ nyã na maala a lɛ a n Sãa Nãaŋmɩn na be a saaju na bʊɔrɔ.
translated: They should not think that because they call on the Lord, the Lord, that they will receive God's blessing, because the person who loves him is the one whom the Holy Spirit will come to help and save.

ref: Suddenly they shouted, “Jesus, Son of God, what do you want with us? Have you come to punish us before our time?”
text: Ban wa nyɛ a Yesu ba ŋmen chɛlma tɩ 'yɛrɛ, “Bo lɛ fʊ bʊɔrɔ ka a sɩ sɛ̃, fʊ̃ʊ Nãaŋmɩn Bie? Fʊ wan ɩka fʊ wa ge sɩ tɩchɛ ka a 'yɛr tɔɔrfʊ bɩbir wa ta bɩɩ?”
translated: When they saw Jesus walking on the water, they said, "Why are you afraid and doubting, don't you have faith in God? Don't you know that you should have faith so that he can help you in your troubles?"

ref: Jesus heard them and answered, “Healthy people don't need a doctor, but sick people do.
text: Yesu wõn a lɛ ban 'yɛr, ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, “Nɩrɛ na kpɛ̃mɛ ba chiine tɩtɩlɛ jie ɛ, tɩchɛ baalaa lɛ ma chen.
translated: Jesus answered them saying, "Truly I tell you, you seek me not because you saw signs, but because you ate the bread and were satisfied."

ref: But Jesus knew what was in their minds, and he said, “Why are you thinking such evil things?
text: Yesu bɔ̃ɔn a lɛ ban tɩɛr, ka wʊ sʊʊr ba, “Ŋmɩnɛ lɛ yɩ tɩɛrɛ dɛɛr a yɩ pʊʊr pɔ?
translated: Jesus asked them a question, and he said to them, "Who do people say that I am?"

ref: As Jesus was leaving that place, two blind men began following him and shouting, “Son of David, have pity on us!”
text: Yesu na wa yi a be chiine, jɔ̃n ayi turo wʊ naa, na ba gboolo bʊɔlɔ, “Kaa sɩ nɩbaal, David Bie.”
translated: When Jesus was passing by them, they heard it and they both shouted, "Have mercy on us, Son of David."

ref: Jesus sent out the twelve apostles with these instructions: Stay away from the Gentiles and don't go to any Samaritan town.
text: A poturbo pie nɩ ayi banyã lɛ a Yesu tʊ̃ nɩ a kpãafʊ anyãna. “Yɩ ta kpɛ a nɩyor bɩɩ a Samariya tẽn pɔ ɛ,
translated: They were surprised that he was talking with a woman. "Do not enter any town of the Samaritans,

ref: But you will not really be the one speaking. The Spirit from your Father will tell you what to say.
text: A ba ɩ yɩn lɛ na 'yɛr ɛ tɩchɛ a yɩ Sãa Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ na wiil yɩn a lɛ yɩn na 'yɛr.
translated: They do not want to accept that it is God's Spirit that is helping them to accept.

ref: Don't think I came to bring peace to the earth! I came to bring trouble, not peace.
text: “Yɩ ta tɩɛr ɩka nyã'maar lɛ n waan a wɛr pɔ ɛ; n ba waan nyã'maar ɛ tɩchɛ ootaa lɛ n waan.
translated: If you want to do something good and you don't have the strength for it; don't do the bad thing instead that you can do.

ref: Anyone who welcomes a prophet, just because that person is a prophet, will be given the same reward as a prophet. Anyone who welcomes a good person, just because that person is good, will be given the same reward as a good person.
text: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa na sɔɔ de a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ wʊn ɩ Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ jũu na nyɛn a wʊ sãyar. Nɩrɛ jaa mɩ na sɔɔ de nɩrɛ na ɩ nɩtor wʊn ɩ nɩtor jũu na nyɛn a nɩtor sãyar.
translated: A person should not love God's things and God's possessions more than his soul. A person should not love things and wealth and riches more than his soul.

ref: As John's followers were going away, Jesus spoke to the crowds about John: What sort of person did you go out into the desert to see? Was he like tall grass blown about by the wind?
text: Ban wa chen baar, Yesu piel 'yɛrɛ a Jɔɔn 'yɛr kʊrɔ a nɩyɔɔ, “Bo lɛ yɩ dɔ̃ɔ kpɛ a mɔgɔ̃ɔ pɔ ɩka yɩ tɩ kaa? A jɩmur a sɛsɛbɛ na dɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ bɩɩ?
translated: When they had gone, Jesus began to speak to the crowd about John, "What did you go out into the wilderness to see? A reed shaken by the wind?

ref: John was in prison when he heard what Christ was doing. So John sent some of his followers
text: Jɔɔn na be a nɩpɔɔ die pɔ, tɩ wa wõ lɛ a Yesu Kɩrɩsɩto na maala, wʊ tʊ̃n a wʊ poturbo bamɩne
translated: John wants to tell people that Jesus Christ has come, so he writes to his friends

ref: God will bless everyone who doesn't reject me because of what I do.”
text: Pʊpɛl sʊɔ lɛ a 'lɔ na ba baala ma ɛ.”
translated: The blind person says he will not go to the farm again.

ref: And if you believe them, John is Elijah, the prophet you are waiting for.
text: Ala ɩ yɩn sɔɔn de, a anyãna 'lɔ lɛ ɩ a Elaija nɩ naa lɩɛb wa.
translated: But if you want to accept it, he is Elijah who was to come.

ref: People of Capernaum, do you think you will be honored in heaven? You will go down to hell! If the miracles that took place in your town had happened in Sodom, it would still be standing.
text: A yɩn Kapenahum dẽme, ban 'mɔɔ yɩn don saaju bɩɩ? Kai, ban suun yɩn a kpĩisɩ tẽe. Ala ɩ a nɛ'maa tʊ̃mɔ̃ na tʊ̃ a yɩ pɔ mɩ ta tʊ̃n a Sodom pɔ, a tẽe anyãna naa chɛnɛ na wa tɔ dɩna.
translated: And you Capernaum, will you be lifted up to heaven? No, you will be brought down to Hades. For if the mighty works done in you had been done in Sodom, it would have remained until today.

ref: Jesus left and went into one of their synagogues,
text: Yesu yin a be chen a ba lɔ̃ɔfʊ yir pɔ,
translated: Jesus went and entered into his Father's house,

text: A pɔɔ nãa na yin be a par a 'yɛr tɔɔrfʊ daar na wʊ wa 'yɔ̃ɔ a yɩn dɩna nɩbɛ 'yɛr, bojũu wʊ dɔ̃ɔ yi be a tẽtɛɛr wa chɛl a Nãa Solomon yɛ̃ 'yɛr, tɩchɛ yɩ kaa a 'lɔ na gar a Solomon ben ka.
translated: And if God so clothes the grass of the field, which today is alive and tomorrow is thrown into the oven, will he not much more clothe you, O you of little faith?

ref: Many prophets and good people were eager to see what you see and to hear what you hear. But I tell you they did not see or hear. Jesus Explains the Story about the Farmer (Mark
text: Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ yɔɔ nɩ nɩtorsɩ yɔɔ ta bʊɔrɔ na ɩka ba nyɛ bon 'lɔ yɩn nyɛ tɩchɛ baa nyɛ ɛ, na ba ta wõ bon 'lɔ yɩn wõ tɩchɛ baa tʊ̃ɔ wõ ɛ.
translated: The world and its fullness belong to God, both people and animals, so that they should do what is good and not do what is bad, and they should say what is good and not say what is bad.

ref: The seeds that fell among the thornbushes are also people who hear the message. But they start worrying about the needs of this life and are fooled by the desire to get rich. So the message gets choked out, and they never produce anything.
text: A bʊ̃bie ala mɩ na lo a gʊʊr pɔ, 'lɔ lɛ a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na wõ a 'yɛr tɩ nʊ̃ a wɛr ju nyɛvʊr yelwõnsɩ nɩ a wɛr ju bʊ̃bʊɔrsɩ fãɩn a 'yɛr ka waa dɔɔ bie ɛ.
translated: They should also tell their children that when they grow up and have their own children, they should teach their children good behavior and their children should follow their example so that they too will become good people.

ref: After Jesus left the crowd and went inside, his disciples came to him and said, “Explain to us the story about the weeds in the wheat field.”
text: Wʊ yin a nɩyɔɔ pɔ kpɛ a yir, ka a wʊ poturbo wa a wʊ sɛ̃ wa 'yɛrɛ, wiil sɩ a mɔɩ na be a pʊɔ pɔ sʊkpa par.
translated: If you see a snake in your house, and you don't have a cutlass or a stick nearby, then you should call your neighbor to come and help you kill it.

ref: Weeds are gathered and burned. That's how it will be at the end of time.
text: “Lɛ ban na chʊm a mɔɩ bũu pɔ ka a di, lɛ mɩ lɛ na ɩ a wɛr baarfʊ daar.
translated: If they do not want to give you food to eat, then I will take you to your father's house.

ref: The people found out he was there, and they sent word to everyone who lived in this part of the country. So they brought all the sick people to Jesus.
text: A tẽe 'lɔ dẽme na wa wõ a wʊ yele ba 'yɛr lʊɔr a paal 'lɔ wʊ jaa na ba waan bala jaa an ɔɔrɔ,
translated: If you do not want to be like those who have become lost on the path, you should follow those who have gone before you and returned,

ref: Jesus answered: Why do you disobey God and follow your own teaching?
text: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, “Ŋmɩnɛ ka yɩ sɔ̃ɔ a Nãaŋmɩn nɛbinsɩ tɩ na yɩ turo a yɩ mãɛ wiilfʊ?
translated: And Jesus asked them, "Which is easier, to say to the paralytic 'Your sins are forgiven' or to say 'Get up and walk'?"

ref: Stay away from those Pharisees! They are like blind people leading other blind people, and all of them will fall into a ditch.”
text: Yɩ bar ba, jɔ̃n lɛ tɔɔrɔ jɔ̃n. Jɔ̃ɔ wa tɔɔrɔ a wʊ jɔ̃ɔ tɔ sʊɔ ba jaa ma lon bɔɔ pɔ.”
translated: If they refuse, the matter will remain a matter. The matter will remain until the person who started the matter comes to settle it.

ref: Large crowds came and brought many people who were paralyzed or blind or lame or unable to talk. They placed them, and many others, in front of Jesus, and he healed them all.
text: A nɩyɔɔ tara na a nɩbɛ gbɛɛ na kpi kpi, nɩ jɔ̃n, nɩ a gbɛrsɩ, nɩ a wõn 'matãan baalsɩ yɔɔ waan a wʊ sɛ̃. Ba wa bin ban a wʊ niem a wʊ sãa a ba jaa baalsɩ.
translated: If a person's wife gives birth to many children, both boys and girls, and they grow up, when that person dies they will bury him in his house. They will remove the roof of his house so they can bury him there.

ref: Jesus replied, “It isn't right to take food away from children and feed it to dogs.”
text: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr, “A ba sɛɛ ɩka fʊ de bibiir bʊ̃diir lɔb 'yɔ̃ɔ baar ɛ.”
translated: And Jesus said, "You will see the heavens opened and the angels of God ascending and descending."

ref: From there, Jesus went along Lake Galilee. Then he climbed a hill and sat down.
text: Yesu na wa yi a be, wʊ den a Galilee man yal kõkoor 'lana, na wʊ tɩ do tɔ̃ɔ ju jãnɛ.
translated: Jesus came and found them, he then left the Galilee region secretly, and he did not want anyone to know.

ref: His disciples said, “This place is like a desert. Where can we find enough food to feed such a crowd?”
text: Ka a wʊ poturbo 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, “Nyɛ lɛ sɩn nyɛ paanʊ̃ɔ a dãlpʊɔ nyã pɔ tʊ̃ɔ kãan a nɩyɔɔ banyãna?”
translated: And you will ask them saying, "Why did you not bring the prisoner to the court today as I commanded you?"

ref: Don't you know by now that I am not talking to you about bread? Watch out for the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees!
text: Bo lɛ so ka yaa tɩɛr bɔ̃ɔ ɩka a ba ɩ paanʊ̃ɔ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ ɛ? Yɩ gu yɩ mãɛ a Faraseemɩne nɩ a Saduseemɩne dãbɩl yele pɔ.”
translated: Do you not yet understand or remember the five loaves for the five thousand and how many baskets you gathered? Or the seven loaves for the four thousand and how many baskets you gathered? How is it that you do not understand that I was not speaking to you about bread? But beware of the leaven of the Pharisees and Sadducees.

ref: The disciples talked this over and said to each other, “He must be saying this because we didn't bring along any bread.”
text: Ba piel mana kʊrɔ taa, “Sɩn ba waan paanʊ̃ɔ jaa lɛ so.”
translated: They asked the old man, "Why did they beat the child?"

ref: So I will call you Peter, which means “a rock.” On this rock I will build my church, and death itself will not have any power over it.
text: Maa lɛ 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ fʊ, a fʊ̃ʊ Pita, a par lɛ kʊsɩr pie, a fʊ 'yɛr nyãna pɔ lɛ, ɩn tu bʊɔl nɩbɛ a ba wa sɔɔ de ma ka a kpĩi tẽe kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ ba ɛ.
translated: I will call you Peter, which means rock, and I will build my church on you, and the powers of death will not be able to overcome it.

ref: If the follower refuses to listen to them, report the matter to the church. Anyone who refuses to listen to the church must be treated like an unbeliever or a tax collector. Allowing and Not Allowing
text: 'Lɔ wa tɔr a nɩbɛ bala niem, 'yɛr kʊ a pupuorbiir, ka 'lɔ wa chɛnɛ tɔr na waa tu a ba nɛɛ ɛ, fʊ maal wʊ fɩka a lɛ fʊn na maal a nɩyuo, bɩɩ a lõpodier kaar.
translated: If you want to help someone, don't just give them things, but if you want to truly help them so they can stand on their own, teach them how to do it themselves so they can teach their children, or their grandchildren later.

ref: I am sure that finding it would make you happier than having the
text: Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ, 'lɔ wa nyɛ wʊ, nʊ̃ɔ ma kpɛ wʊ naa gar a kɔbaa forbir ala na ba bɔr ɛ.
translated: When a person is sick, if they don't get well, people will take them to the hospital so that they can treat them.

ref: A man came to Jesus and asked, “Teacher, what good thing must I do to have eternal life?”
text: ) na fʊ mɩ lɛ nʊ̃ a fʊ tɔ sʊɔ a fʊ mãɛ kaar.” (Mosesɩ Gãma Na Tu Ata
translated: and you will give me a person and you will take the animal and you will give it to the priest." (The Law of Moses)

ref: The young man said, “I have obeyed all of these. What else must I do?”
text: Ka a daba 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, “N tu naa anyãna a jaa, bo lɛ chɛ ma?”
translated: If you ask the child, "I am going to the market tomorrow, will you follow me?"

ref: Jesus looked straight at them and said, “There are some things people cannot do, but God can do anything.”
text: Ka Yesu kaa ba tɩ 'yɛr, “Ala na ɩ tuo nɩrɛ sɛ̃, Nãaŋmɩn sɛ̃ yele jaa ba ɩ tuo ɛ.”
translated: And Jesus said to them, "If it were not so, would I have told you that I go to prepare a place for you?"

ref: Then Jesus left the city and went out to the village of Bethany, where he spent the night. Jesus Puts a Curse on a Fig Tree (Mark
text: Wʊ bar ba naa tɩ yi chen a Betanɩ tẽe pɔ tɩ gã a be.
translated: They did not want him to pass through Bethany again to meet them.

ref: Tell us what you think! Should we pay taxes to the Emperor or not?”
text: 'Yɛr kʊ sɩ a lɛ fʊn tɩɛr. A tara na sɔr ɩka sɩ yab a lõpoo kʊ a Nãkpɛ̃ɛ Siisa bɩɩ sɩ ta yab ɛ?”
translated: "Is it not you who should be afraid? Why do you think that your grandfather and Nãkpɛ̃ɛ Siisa would be afraid of him?"

ref: And as for people being raised to life, God was speaking to you when he said,
text: Tɩchɛ a kũu pɔ ir fʊ yele, yaa kar a lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn na dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr kʊ yɩ ɩka,
translated: If you do not believe what he says, then know that God will punish you one day,

ref: While the Pharisees were still there, Jesus asked them,
text: A Faraseemɩne na wa 'mataa, Yesu sʊʊr ba naa.
translated: When the Pharisees heard this, Jesus told them.

ref: Jerusalem, Jerusalem! Your people have killed the prophets and have stoned the messengers who were sent to you. I have often wanted to gather your people, as a hen gathers her chicks under her wings. But you wouldn't let me.
text: “Pa yɩn Jerusalɛm nɩbɛ, a yɩn na ma kʊrɔ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ na yɩ lɔɔrɔ a bala a Nãaŋmɩn na tʊ̃ a yɩ sɛ̃ nɩ kʊsɩbɛ, nyɛ lɛ an ma ɩrɛ ma vɔlaa jã ka n ma bʊɔrɔ ɩka n kaal a fʊ bibiir 'mataa fɩka nʊ̃ɔ na ma ũun a wʊ bil, tɩchɛ ka faa bʊɔrɔ ɩka fʊ maal a lɛ ɛ.
translated: When you are in Jerusalem, you will see my Father's worshippers who are there worshipping the Father in spirit and in truth, then you will know my words that I spoke to you saying that the time is coming when you will not worship on this mountain or in that place, but will worship in spirit and in truth.

ref: And anyone who swears by the temple also swears by God, who lives there.
text: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ mɩ na pɔn a Nãaŋmɩn yir, pɔn wʊ naa nɩ a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na jãnɛ a be.
translated: If you do not enter God's house, enter it so that you may know the people.

ref: I can promise that you people living today will be punished for all these things! Jesus Loves Jerusalem (Luke
text: Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ, a yɩn dɩna nɩbɛ na nyɛn gefʊ a yele anyãna jũu.
translated: When a person is sick, they should go to the hospital so that the doctor can examine their body.

ref: You won't see me again until you say, “Blessed is the one who comes in the name of the Lord.”
text: Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka yɩ kʊ̃ lɛ nyɛ ma go ɛ, wa tɩ tãn yɩn na 'yɛr ɩka, ‘Nãaŋmɩn wʊ sʊ̃ʊ a 'lɔ wʊn tʊ̃.’ ” (Yiel Gãn
translated: If a person does not have money to pay what he owes, he should tell his creditor, 'God willing, I will pay you tomorrow.'

ref: Someday you will see that “Horrible Thing” in the holy place, just as the prophet Daniel said. Everyone who reads this must try to understand!
text: “Yɩn wa nyɛ naa a yele 'lɔ a Nãaŋmɩn na 'laar na waara nɩ sɔ̃ɔfʊ ka wʊ be a jichɛchɛ 'lɔ nɩtãa lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrɛ Danɩɛl na dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr. Vɛ̃ ka a nyã na kara bɔ̃ɔ a par. (Danɩɛl
translated: If you do this, then you will be like the God who protected and saved from death those three young men whom God's servant Daniel prayed for. So that your name will be great in the land. (Daniel

ref: If you are out in the field, don't go back for your coat.
text: 'Lɔ mɩ na be a puo pɔ wʊ ta kul a yir tɩ de a wʊ fuu ɛ.
translated: If I say that your child is bad, it is because he has spoiled your name.

ref: You may be sure a servant who is always faithful will be put in charge of everything the master owns.
text: Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ, wʊn vɛ̃n ka wʊ kaara a wʊ bonsɩ a jaa.
translated: When the child is still small, they don't know their right from their left.

ref: But the groom replied, “I don't even know you!”
text: “Tɩchɛ ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, ‘Yelmãɛ jaa, n ba bɔ̃ɔ yɩ ɛ.’
translated: "The person said to his friend, 'God willing, I will come tomorrow.'"

ref: “Wonderful!” his master replied. “You are a good and faithful servant. I left you in charge of only a little, but now I will put you in charge of much more. Come and share in my happiness!”
text: “Ka a wʊ nɩkpɛ̃ɛ 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, ‘Fʊ tʊ̃n vɩla, gbãgbamãɛ lɛ fʊ ɩ. Fʊ tara na yelmãɛ a bʊ̃bile pɔ, ɩn 'yɔ̃ɔ fʊn ka fʊ kaara bʊ̃yɔɔ. Wa pʊɔ ma ka sɩ 'mataa dire a nʊ̃ɔ.’
translated: And if someone says to you, 'You are still young, you are a child.' You should answer the elders and the people that, 'I have come so that you may receive salvation. God has sent me so that you may enter heaven. That is why I have come to this place.'

ref: Everyone who has something will be given more, and they will have more than enough. But everything will be taken from those who don't have anything.
text: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa na tara yɔɔ, ban kʊ wʊn yɔɔ pʊɔ ka ba tara yɔɔ tɩchɛ a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na ba tara bon jaa ɛ, a bãlãa 'lɔ wʊn tara gba ban faan yin a wʊ sɛ̃.
translated: When that person comes to the farm, they will not be able to farm because that person they are farming for is not there, and the owner will farm all their fields by themselves.

ref: I was a stranger, but you did not welcome me, and I was naked, but you did not give me any clothes to wear. I was sick and in jail, but you did not take care of me.”
text: N dɔ̃ɔ ɩ sãan, ka yaa kaa ma sãan kaa fʊ ɛ, n ba tara bon jaa na su ɛ, ka yaa kʊ ma fuu ka su ɛ, ka n bɩɛrɛ, bɩɩ be pɔɔfʊ die pɔ, ka yaa wa piir ma kaa ɛ.’
translated: If I find favor in your sight, and if it pleases you, I will not pass by your servant, and if it pleases you to give me water, and I will wash, because they are tired from the journey, and you will give me food to eat.

ref: When Jesus was eating with his twelve disciples that evening, he said, “One of you will surely hand me over to my enemies.”
text: Ban wa dire, wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, “Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka, a yɩ ãsʊɔ na gar 'yɔ̃ɔ ma.”
translated: They will say, you will hear them say, "God has spoken to me in a dream, he has shown me something in a vision."

ref: Peter spoke up, “Even if all the others reject you, I never will!”
text: Ka a Pita 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, “Ala ɩ ba jaa sɔɔn bar fʊ a fʊ jũu, maa 'lɔ kʊ̃ bar fʊ ɛ.”
translated: And Peter answered him, "Even if all the others deny you, I will never deny you."

ref: He came back and found his disciples sleeping. So he said to Peter, “Can't any of you stay awake with me for just one hour?
text: Wʊ lɩɛb wa a poturbo sɛ̃ wa nyɛ ka ba guro. Ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, “A mɔ̃ yɩ kʊ̃ tʊ̃ɔ jɛ̃ bãlãa jaa gu ma ɛ?
translated: Did you not hear that the Portuguese said that they would not come? And you asked them, "Is it not true that you will come to help us?"

ref: A little while later some people standing there walked over to Peter and said, “We know you are one of them. We can tell it because you talk like someone from Galilee.”
text: A ba kor ɛ, ka a bala na ara gbʊr a Pita mɩ 'yɛr, “Sɩrɛ jaa lɛ, fʊ ɩ naa a ba ãsʊɔ. A fʊ kɔkɔr yin fʊn chãa pɔ.”
translated: When they had finished eating, he asked Simon Peter, "Simon son of John, do you love me more than these?"

ref: On the way they met a man named Simon who was from Cyrene, and they forced him to carry Jesus' cross.
text: Ban wa yi a sɔr pɔ, ba nyɛn daba na yi Sirenɩ ka a wʊ yuor di Simon, ka ba nyɔɔ wʊ fɩr wʊ ka wʊ tuo a Yesu daa.
translated: As they were going out, they found a man from Cyrene whose name was Simon, and they forced him to carry his cross for Jesus.

ref: This is why people still call that place “Field of Blood.”
text: Lɛ lɛ so ka nɩbɛ bʊɔlɔ a jie nyã, “Jãɩ Wɛr,” wa tãn dɩna.
translated: And if someone asks you your name, say, "I am Wɛr," and come back.

ref: He trusted God, so let God save him, if he wants to. He even said he was God's Son.”
text: Wʊ sɔɔn Nãaŋmɩn. Vɛ̃ ka Nãaŋmɩn faa wʊ a pʊ̃pãanyãna, bojũu wʊ 'yɛr naa ɩka wʊ ɩn Nãaŋmɩn Bie.”
translated: You fear God. But God did not give you understanding, so that you would know that you are in God's House.

ref: The officer and the soldiers guarding Jesus felt the earthquake and saw everything else that happened. They were frightened and said, “This man really was God's Son!”
text: A sojasɩ nɩkpɛ̃ɛ nɩ a bala wʊn ara nɩ na ba pire a Yesu, nyɛn a wɛr na dãm nɩ a lɛ jaa na ɩ, ka dãbãɛ kpɛ ba, ka ba 'yɛr, “Yelmãɛ jaa lɛ, a daba nyã ɩn a Nãaŋmɩn Bie.”
translated: The soldiers also mocked him and came to him offering him sour wine, saying, "If you are the King of the Jews, save yourself."

ref: On the next day, which was a Sabbath, the chief priests and the Pharisees went together to Pilate.
text: A Chɔɔrfʊ Bɩbir ka a wɛr na chaar ka a ɩ a Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir lɛ, a Juu nɩbɛ bɔɔrlo nɩbɛrɛ nɩ a Faraseemɩne chen tɩ 'yɛr a Nãkpɛ̃ɛ Pilate,
translated: The Chief Priests and the elders and the Pharisees went to the Governor Pilate,

ref: Jesus' eleven disciples went to a mountain in Galilee, where Jesus had told them to meet him.
text: A poturbo pie nɩ bʊ̃'yen chen a Galilee a tɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ ju a Yesu na wiil ba ɩka ba do.
translated: The disciples who were with him from Galilee followed and saw the tomb where his body was laid.

ref: So the chief priests met with the leaders and decided to bribe the soldiers with a lot of money.
text: A bɔɔrlo nãa nɩ a wʊ nɩbɛrɛ na wa lɔ̃ɔ taa, ba guor taa de libiyɔɔ kʊ a sojasɩ
translated: If your child and your grandchild want to go to war, they will put the responsibility on your soldier

ref: It began just as God had said in the book written by Isaiah the prophet, “I am sending my messenger to get the way ready for you.
text: 'Yɛ'yɛrɛ kɔ̃ɔ yuor na di Isaiya sɛb naa, “Nyɛ, ɩn tʊ̃ naa a n tʊ̃tʊ̃nɔ̃ ka wʊ de nie tɩ chɔɔr a sɔr kʊ fʊ.” (Malakaɩ
translated: This matter happened so that what the prophet Isaiah said would be fulfilled, "See, I will send my messenger ahead of you to prepare your way before you." (Malachi

ref: Everyone was amazed at his teaching. He taught with authority, and not like the teachers of the Law of Moses.
text: A nɩbɛ nɛɛr 'maa naa nɩ a wʊ wiilfʊ, bojũu wʊ wile ba naa nɩ Nãaŋmɩn sɔr. Waa ɩ fɩka a Mosesɩ Nɛɛ wiwiilbe ɛ.
translated: If you do not do what you have promised, then you will have sinned against the Lord. And you will find that your sin will punish you.

ref: Jesus told the evil spirit, “Be quiet and come out of the man!”
text: Ka a Yesu tãn wʊ, “Pɔɔ a fʊ nɛɛ tɩ wa yi a daba pɔ!”
translated: And Jesus said to him, "Stretch out your hand so that it may be healed!"

ref: Jesus answered, “Haven't you read what David did when he and his followers were hungry and in need?
text: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, “Yaa bɔ̃ɔ a par a lɛ yɩn kar kaa a Nãaŋmɩn gãn pɔ a nãa David nɩ a wʊ nɩbɛ na ba dɔ̃ɔ tara bon na di ɛ!
translated: When Jesus heard them, he said, "Yes, because you have hidden these things from the wise and learned, and revealed them to little children!"

ref: So the Son of Man is Lord over the Sabbath.”
text: Ala lɛ so ka a Nɩsaal Bie ɩ a Soro lɔ̃ɔn a Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir gba.”
translated: But if you do not obey the voice of the Lord your God to keep all his commandments

ref: The Pharisees wanted to accuse Jesus of doing something wrong, and they kept watching to see if Jesus would heal him on the Sabbath.
text: Ka a nɩbɛ bamɩne na be be bʊɔrɔ ɩka ba gar a Yesu, be lɛ ba tɔɔ gbʊr kaara wʊ. Ka wʊn sãa naa a daba nũu baal a Pɛ̃nfʊ Bɩbir bɩɩ.
translated: And his disciples were amazed that he was speaking with a woman, but no one said "What do you seek?" or "Why are you talking with her?" And the woman left her water pot there and went to the town.

ref: Jesus asked, “Who is my mother and who are my brothers?”
text: Ka wʊ sʊʊr ba, “An lɛ ɩ a n ma nɩ a n yɛɛr?”
translated: "And you ask him, "What is it that you want me to do for you?""

ref: He used stories to teach them many things, and this is part of what he taught:
text: Sʊkpar lɛ wʊ lɔɔrɔ wile nɩ ba yelyɔɔ, sʊkpa ãsʊɔ nɩ nyã:
translated: When a man marries a woman and they give birth, the child resembles him:

ref: It is the ground that makes the seeds sprout and grow into plants that produce grain.
text: A bʊ̃bie ma bul naa a tẽe naa tɔ vaar tɩ dɔɔ jur, bɩ a bie.
translated: They don't want their children or their fathers to go to war and die, it is their children.

ref: Suddenly a storm struck the lake. Waves started splashing into the boat, and it was about to sink.
text: Ka a man kʊ̃ɔ sɛkpɛ̃ɛ 'lɔ ire ka a kʊ̃ɔ kpɛ a gboro pɔ ɩrɛ na sɛɛ
translated: If you do not know how to do something, then you should not do it at all or else it will not be good

ref: She had gone to many doctors, and they had not done anything except cause her a lot of pain. She had paid them all the money she had. But instead of getting better, she only got worse.
text: Wʊ bal di dɔɔyɛ yɔɔ a tɩtɩɩlbɛ jie na wʊ sɔ̃ɔ bon jaa wʊn tara tɩ ba sa ɛ. Ka a baal tʊ̃ɔnɔ̃ wʊ.
translated: They will not be able to see the light and they will not be able to walk because they have fallen. So the darkness has covered them.

ref: Night and day he was in the graveyard or on the hills, yelling and cutting himself with stones.
text: Tãsɔɔ nɩ mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ, wʊ ma be naa a yar par nɩ a tɔ̃n ju tãna tɩ ŋmara a wʊ mãɛ nɩ kʊsɩbɛ.
translated: In the morning and evening, you should give him his food and his drink so that he may have strength and health.

ref: He then begged Jesus not to send them away.
text: Ka wʊ sʊɔr a Yesu yãayãabɩlɛ ɩka wʊ ta dii wʊ bar a jie 'lɔ ɛ.
translated: If you believe in Jesus' name you will receive what you ask for.

ref: In the crowd was a woman who had been bleeding for twelve years.
text: Pɔɔ kɔ̃ɔ mɩ ben be lo sɩɛ suoro faara yuomo pie nɩ ayi.
translated: The man said that they should go and buy new clothes for the children today.

ref: While Jesus was still speaking, some people came from Jairus' home and said, “Your daughter has died! Why bother the teacher anymore?”
text: A Yesu ba 'yɛr baar ɛ, tɩchɛ nɩbɛ kɔ̃ɔ yi a Jairusɩ yir wa 'yɛr wʊ, “A fʊ bie kpi naa. Bãa 'yɔ̃ɔ fʊ dɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a wiwile go?”
translated: While Jesus was still speaking, someone came from Jairus's house and said, "Your daughter has died. Why trouble the teacher anymore?"

ref: Later in the evening he was still there by himself, and the boat was somewhere in the middle of the lake.
text: A wɛr na wa sɔɔrɔ, a gboro be naa a man sãlsɔɔ, tɩchɛ ka wʊ be a wɛyõo, 'lɔ tɛɛ.
translated: If you want to be rich, you should not be lazy, because it is not in your interest, it is not good.

ref: In every village or farm or marketplace where Jesus went, the people brought their sick to him. They begged him to let them just touch his clothes, and everyone who did was healed.
text: Jie jaa wʊn chen, tẽkpɛ̃ɛ pɔ bɩɩ, tẽbile pɔ bɩɩ, ka a tẽe kõkoor lɛ̃ɛɛ, ba ma gaal naa a ba baalsɩ a chãa pɔ. Ba ma sʊɔrɔ naa a Yesu ɩka wʊ sɔɔ ka ba sɩɩr a wʊ fuu nɛɛ gba, bala jaa na sɩɩr, ma sa naa.
translated: When that day came, when evening fell, when the sun had set, and darkness had covered everywhere, they brought to him all their sick people. They brought to Jesus so that he could lay his hands on all of them, because whoever he laid hands on would be healed.

ref: But you let people get by without helping their parents when they should. You let them say that what they own has been offered to God.
text: Tɩchɛ ka yɩn 'yɛr ɩka a sɛɛ na, nɩrɛ wa 'yɛr kʊ a wʊ sãa bɩɩ a wʊ ma, ‘Bon jaa ɩn naa kʊ fʊ, n den kʊ Nãaŋmɩn.’
translated: If someone does not love his brother whom he has seen, he cannot love God whom he has not seen.

ref: When the woman got back home, she found her child lying on the bed. The demon had gone. Jesus Heals a Man Who Was Deaf and Could Hardly Talk
text: Wʊ kul tɩ nyɛ a bie na gã a gado ju, tɩ a sɩdɛɛr yi.
translated: He refused to give his child to the white man, saying it was his responsibility.

ref: At once the man could hear, and he had no more trouble talking clearly.
text: Ajienaa ka a daba tobo yuo ka a jãlbir lɩɛb ka wʊ piel 'yɛrɛ jaa chɛchɛ.
translated: The woman took her husband's money and bought cloth and they sewed a beautiful dress.

ref: Jesus warned them, “Watch out! Guard against the yeast of the Pharisees and of Herod.”
text: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, “Yɩ bɔ̃ɔn a Faraseemɩne paanʊ̃ɔ dãbɩl nɩ a Hɛrɔd paanʊ̃ɔ dãbɩl mɩ.”
translated: And Jesus said to them, "Beware of the yeast of the Pharisees and the yeast of Herod."

ref: After Jesus told the crowd to sit down, he took the seven loaves and gave thanks. He then broke the loaves and handed them to his disciples, who passed them out to the crowd.
text: Wʊ 'yɛr naa a nɩbɛ, ka ba jãnɛ tẽe, tɩ na wʊ de a paanʊ̃ɔsɩ ayopõi ala puor Nãaŋmɩn yãan, tɩ ŋma ŋma de kʊ a wʊ poturbo ka ba de põ a nɩbɛ.
translated: If you love your neighbor, and they do evil, then you should give them good advice or pray to God for them, so that each person can help their friend and they can do good to their neighbor.
